generatedSentence: From his armchair, a solitary gardener meticulously transcribed historical accounts in longhand, pondering the true meaning of a peculiar, wobbly bracket. He abhorred the flashy leniency shown toward neglect, especially concerning the unique jawfish in his dusty vitrine, and the worrisome puberty affecting local dingoes, often feeding them nibblies as a twosome. This morning, a fateful violation emerged: a massive, porky umbrellabird with knobbed kneecap bones, outmatched by a cunning coyote, had been evicted from its den. The gardener, now with a womanish sense of dread, felt blame invoked, as if his abstract studies and a nearby turntable's incessant rhapsody somehow related to this bloody setback and the unsettling sight of a vagrant cougar’s jaws. He grabbed lighters and a hand-held vidicon, determined to upgrade his defense; the widespread virus, an abstract graphic on his finder, was clearly the payoff for some demented gambling. Then, a flashbang exploded, creating thunder as bullets flew toward a narrow waist, forcing him to bashfuly kowtow behind a flabby chicken while the notary and clergy, like cannibals, stood by, absolving themselves of any responsibility. In the brilliant sunlight, the air clear after the nocturn, he emerged, his improved iceboat now hitched and unlocked, reflecting his enthusiastic new mantra. He verbally rebuffed the idea of further conflict, instead offering toffee and batata, having finally fished a solution from the flyways. He understood the profound chemistry of peace, an enduring ownership more valuable than any jewelry, proving veracity beyond words.
panelSentences: From his armchair, a solitary gardener meticulously transcribed historical accounts in longhand, pondering the true meaning of a peculiar, wobbly bracket. He abhorred the flashy leniency shown toward neglect, especially concerning the unique jawfish in his dusty vitrine, and the worrisome puberty affecting local dingoes, often feeding them nibblies as a twosome.

This morning, a fateful violation emerged: a massive, porky umbrellabird with knobbed kneecap bones, outmatched by a cunning coyote, had been evicted from its den. The gardener, now with a womanish sense of dread, felt blame invoked, as if his abstract studies and a nearby turntable's incessant rhapsody somehow related to this bloody setback and the unsettling sight of a vagrant cougar’s jaws.

He grabbed lighters and a hand-held vidicon, determined to upgrade his defense; the widespread virus, an abstract graphic on his finder, was clearly the payoff for some demented gambling. Then, a flashbang exploded, creating thunder as bullets flew toward a narrow waist, forcing him to bashfuly kowtow behind a flabby chicken while the notary and clergy, like cannibals, stood by, absolving themselves of any responsibility.

In the brilliant sunlight, the air clear after the nocturn, he emerged, his improved iceboat now hitched and unlocked, reflecting his enthusiastic new mantra. He verbally rebuffed the idea of further conflict, instead offering toffee and batata, having finally fished a solution from the flyways. He understood the profound chemistry of peace, an enduring ownership more valuable than any jewelry, proving veracity beyond words.
rawPanelSentences: From his armchair, a solitary gardener meticulously transcribed historical accounts in longhand, pondering the true meaning of a peculiar, wobbly bracket. He abhorred the flashy leniency shown toward neglect, especially concerning the unique jawfish in his dusty vitrine, and the worrisome puberty affecting local dingoes, often feeding them nibblies as a twosome.

This morning, a fateful violation emerged: a massive, porky umbrellabird with knobbed kneecap bones, outmatched by a cunning coyote, had been evicted from its den. The gardener, now with a womanish sense of dread, felt blame invoked, as if his abstract studies and a nearby turntable's incessant rhapsody somehow related to this bloody setback and the unsettling sight of a vagrant cougar’s jaws.

He grabbed lighters and a hand-held vidicon, determined to upgrade his defense; the widespread virus, an abstract graphic on his finder, was clearly the payoff for some demented gambling. Then, a flashbang exploded, creating thunder as bullets flew toward a narrow waist, forcing him to bashfuly kowtow behind a flabby chicken while the notary and clergy, like cannibals, stood by, absolving themselves of any responsibility.

In the brilliant sunlight, the air clear after the nocturn, he emerged, his improved iceboat now hitched and unlocked, reflecting his enthusiastic new mantra. He verbally rebuffed the idea of further conflict, instead offering toffee and batata, having finally fished a solution from the flyways. He understood the profound chemistry of peace, an enduring ownership more valuable than any jewelry, proving veracity beyond words.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lean figure with intricate cybernetic arm enhancements and a glowing ocular implant. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a futuristic armchair, writing with a glowing stylus, looking at a transparent vitrine containing exotic fish, a robotic dingo beside him, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A colossal, mechanical umbrellabird collapsing near a high-tech den entrance, a sleek metallic coyote stalking away, with the protagonist watching from a distance, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist diving behind a futuristic console as laser bullets ricochet around, a holographic notary and clergy figures observing impassively in the background, close-up on protagonist's face. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist triumphantly standing on an upgraded, sleek iceboat on a shimmering, clean waterway at dawn, a peaceful cyborg dingo beside him, wide shot.

definitions.definition: A chair with supports for the arms or elbows.
To create based on theory or general knowledge rather than data.
To theorize based on analysis of data that was gathered previously; to reflect.
Remote from actual involvement, including a person retired from previously active involvement.
definitions.example: These days I'm an armchair detective.

definitions.definition: Unqualified or uninformed but yet giving advice, especially on technical issues, such as law, architecture, medicine, military theory, or sports.
definitions.example: After the American football game, the armchair quarterbacks talked about what they would have done differently to win, if they had been star athletes instead of out-of-shape old men.

definitions.definition: A historical romance.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with recorded history, (particularly) as opposed to legends, myths, and fictions.
definitions.example: July 4, 1776, is a historic date. A great deal of historical research has been done on the events leading up to that day.

definitions.definition: Of, concerning, or in accordance with the past generally.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with the scholarly discipline of history.
definitions.example: The Royal Historical Society

definitions.definition: Forming compound adjectives with the meaning "historical/~" or "historically":
definitions.example: historical-political

definitions.definition: The act or an instance of violating or the condition of being violated.
Showy; visually impressive, attention-getting, or appealing.
definitions.example: The dancers wore flashy costumes featuring shiny sequins in many vibrant colors.

definitions.definition: Flashing; producing flashes.
definitions.example: a flashy light

definitions.definition: Drunk; tipsy
The quality of mercy or forgiveness, especially in the assignment of punishment as in a court case.
An act of being lenient.
To fail to agree; to have a different opinion or belief.
definitions.example: Bob says cats are friendlier than dogs, but I disagree.

definitions.definition: To fail to conform or correspond with.
definitions.example: My results in the laboratory consistently disagree with yours.

definitions.definition: (of words or symbols) The entity, perception, feeling or concept thereby represented or evoked.
The value, purpose, importance, point or significance (of something beyond the fact of that thing's existence).

definitions.definition: The object or concept that a word or phrase denotes, or that which a sentence says.
Intention.
Yielding, pliant; docile; ready or apt to learn; not froward.
Future; to-come.
Approaching, coming near; impending; present, at hand.
Promising, likely.
In the direction of.

definitions.definition: In relation to (someone or something).
definitions.example: What are your feelings toward him?

definitions.definition: For the purpose of attaining (an aim).
definitions.example: I'm saving money toward retirement.

definitions.definition: Located close to; near (a time or place).
definitions.example: Our place is over toward the station.

definitions.definition: An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology
definitions.example: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Up a slope or grade.
A developmental phase brought about by the action of hormones as part of the maturing process. For humans, there are three in total.
The age at which a person is first capable of sexual reproduction.
The period when a plant begins to flower.
A flow of water over the edge of a cliff.
A waterfall-like outpouring of liquid, smoke, etc.
definitions.example: A waterfall of mist came from the open freezer.

definitions.definition: Waterfall model
The action of drinking from a vessel without touching it with the lips, considered more sanitary for a shared vessel.
definitions.example: Hey man, can I take a waterfall from your bottle?

definitions.definition: A necktie.
A chignon.
A beard.
definitions.example: I found home a dreary place after my long absence; for half the children I had known were now wearing whiskers or waterfalls... (Mark Twain, Roughing It)

definitions.definition: To fall like a waterfall.
To drink (something) from a container by pouring it from a height so as not to touch one's lips to the rim.
A member of the Industrial Workers of the World, a militant, radical labor union.
A wobbler; a fit of rage.
Unsteady and tending to wobble.
Causing worry; perturbing or vexing.
(of a person) Inclined to worry.
A fixture attached to a wall to hold up a shelf.
Any intermediate object that connects a smaller part to a larger part, the smaller part typically projecting sideways from the larger part.
A short crooked timber, resembling a knee, used as a support.
The cheek or side of an ordnance carriage, supporting the trunnions.
Any of the characters "(", ")", "[", "]", "{", "}", "<" and ">", used in pairs to enclose parenthetic remarks, sections of mathematical expressions, etc.
A printed diagram of games in a tournament.
A prediction of the outcome of games in a tournament, used for betting purposes.
One of several ranges of numbers.

In artillery, the endangered region between two shell impacts (one long and one short). The next shell fired is likely to hit accurately.
The small curved or angular corner formed by a serif and a stroke in a letter.
(land surveying, 19th century) a mark cut into a stone by land surveyors to secure a bench.
To support by means of mechanical brackets.

definitions.definition: To place in the same category.

definitions.definition: To mark distinctly for special treatment.
To set aside, discount, ignore.
To take multiple images of the same subject, using a range of exposure settings, in order to help ensure that a satisfactory image is obtained.

(phenomenology) In the philosophical system of Edmund Husserl and his followers, to set aside metaphysical theories and existential questions concerning what is real in order to focus philosophical attention simply on the actual content of experience.
Any of many marine fish, of the family Opistognathidae, that have large mouths.
To regard with horror or detestation; to shrink back with shuddering from; to feel excessive repugnance toward; to detest to extremity; to loathe.

The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.

The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.

To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.

An activity characterised by a balance between winning and losing that is governed by a mixture of skill and chance, usually with money wagered on the outcome.

Which forms a surjection from the domain to every open subset of the codomain.

A married person.

Showing commitment or devotion normally reserved for a spouse

definitions.definition: To enter into the conjugal or connubial state; to take a husband or a wife.

definitions.definition: (in passive) To be joined to (someone) as spouse according to law or custom.

definitions.definition: To arrange for the marriage of; to give away as wife or husband.
definitions.example: He was eager to marry his daughter to a nobleman.

definitions.definition: To take as husband or wife.
definitions.example: In some cultures, it is acceptable for an uncle to marry his niece.

definitions.definition: To unite; to join together into a close union.
definitions.example: The attempt to marry medieval plainsong with speed metal produced interesting results.

definitions.definition: To unite in wedlock or matrimony; to perform the ceremony of joining spouses; to bring about a marital union according to the laws or customs of a place.
definitions.example: A justice of the peace will marry Jones and Smith.

definitions.definition: To place (two ropes) alongside each other so that they may be grasped and hauled on at the same time.
To join (two ropes) end to end so that both will pass through a block.
To switch on
definitions.example: Can you on the light?

definitions.definition: In the state of being active, functioning or operating.
definitions.example: All the lights are on, so they must be home.

definitions.definition: Performing according to schedule; taking place.
definitions.example: Are we still on for tonight?

definitions.definition: Fitted; covering; being worn.
definitions.example: I was trying to drink out of the bottle while the top was still on!

definitions.definition: (usually negative) Acceptable, appropriate.
definitions.example: You can't do that; it's just not on.

definitions.definition: Destined, normally in the context of a challenge being accepted; involved, doomed.
definitions.example: "Five bucks says the Cavs win tonight." ―"You're on!"

definitions.definition: Having reached a base as a runner and being positioned there, awaiting further action from a subsequent batter.
Within the half of the field on the same side as the batsman's legs; the left side for a right-handed batsman.
definitions.example: Ponsonby-Smythe hit a thumping on drive.

definitions.definition: Menstruating.
To an operating state.
definitions.example: turn the television on

definitions.definition: So as to cover or be fitted.
definitions.example: Put on your hat and gloves.

definitions.definition: Along, forwards (continuing an action).
definitions.example: drive on, rock on

definitions.definition: In continuation, at length.
definitions.example: He rambled on and on.

definitions.definition: (obsolete in the US) Later.
definitions.example: Ten years on, nothing had changed in the village.

definitions.definition: Of betting odds, denoting a better-than-even chance. See also odds-on.
definitions.example: That horse is twenty-to-one on, so you need to stake twenty pounds just to win one pound.

definitions.definition: Positioned at the upper surface of, touching from above.
definitions.example: A vase of flowers stood on the table.

definitions.definition: Positioned at or resting against the outer surface of; attached to.
definitions.example: He had a scar on the side of his face.

definitions.definition: Expressing figurative placement or attachment.
definitions.example: All of the responsibility is on him.

definitions.definition: Denoting performance or action by contact with the surface, upper part, or outside of anything; hence, by means of; with.
definitions.example: to play on a violin or piano

definitions.definition: At or in (a certain region or location).
definitions.example: The lighthouse that you can see is on the mainland.

definitions.definition: Near; adjacent to; alongside.
definitions.example: Croton-on-Hudson, Rostov-on-Don, Southend-on-Sea

definitions.definition: Supported by (the specified part of itself).
definitions.example: A table can't stand on two legs.

definitions.definition: So as to impart force to.
definitions.example: tug on the rope; push hard on the door

definitions.definition: So as to impact; against.
definitions.example: I stubbed my toe on an old tree stump.

definitions.definition: Covering.
definitions.example: He wore old shoes on his feet.

definitions.definition: (with certain modes of transport, especially public transport) Inside (a vehicle) for the purpose of travelling.
definitions.example: on a bus, on a train, on a plane

definitions.definition: At the date of.
definitions.example: Born on the 4th of July.

definitions.definition: Some time during the day of.
definitions.example: Can I see you on a different day?

definitions.definition: At a given time after the start of something; at.
definitions.example: Smith scored again on twelve minutes, doubling Mudchester Rovers' lead.

definitions.definition: Dealing with the subject of; about; concerning.
definitions.example: I have no opinion on this subject.

definitions.definition: In the possession of.
definitions.example: I haven't got any money on me.

definitions.definition: Because of, or due to.
definitions.example: to arrest someone on suspicion of bribery

definitions.definition: Upon; at the time of (and often because of).
definitions.example: On Jack's entry, William got up to leave.

definitions.definition: Paid for by.
definitions.example: I paid for the airfare and meals for my family, but the hotel room was on the company.

definitions.definition: Indicating a means or medium.
definitions.example: Can't you see I'm on the phone?

definitions.definition: Indicating the target of, or thing affected by, an event or action.
definitions.example: Her words made a lasting impression on my mind.

definitions.definition: Toward; for; indicating the object of an emotion.
definitions.example: Have pity or compassion on him.

definitions.definition: Indicating a means of subsistence.
definitions.example: The dog survived three weeks on rainwater.

definitions.definition: Away or occupied with (e.g. a scheduled activity).
definitions.example: He's on his lunch break.

definitions.definition: Regularly taking (a drug).
definitions.example: He's acting so strangely, I think he must be on something.

definitions.definition: Under the influence of (a drug).
definitions.example: He's acting crazy because he's on crack right now.

definitions.definition: Having identical domain and codomain.
definitions.example: a function on

definitions.definition: Having V^n as domain and V as codomain, for some set V and integer n.
definitions.example: an operator on

definitions.definition: Generated by.
definitions.example: the free group on four letters

definitions.definition: In addition to; besides; indicating multiplication or succession in a series.
definitions.example: heaps on heaps of food

definitions.definition: Of
Indicating dependence or reliance; with confidence in.
definitions.example: He will promise on certain conditions.

definitions.definition: At the peril of, or for the safety of.
In the service of; connected with; of the number of.
definitions.example: He is on a newspaper; I am on the committee.

definitions.definition: By virtue of; with the pledge of.
definitions.example: He affirmed or promised on his word, or on his honour.

definitions.definition: To the account or detriment of; denoting imprecation or invocation, or coming to, falling, or resting upon.
definitions.example: A curse on him!

definitions.definition: (especially when numbers of combatants or competitors are specified) Against; in opposition to.
definitions.example: The fight was three on one, and he never stood a chance.

definitions.definition: A circular rotating platform.
To play (a record) using a turntable.
To rotate or turn around using, or as if using, a turntable.
To manipulate sound using turntables; to perform turntablism.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
definitions.example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definitions.definition: Of a feature, such that only one holder has it.
Particular, characteristic.
Of a rare quality, unusual.
The point of a pin.
To identify or locate precisely or with great accuracy.
definitions.example: The investigators tried to pinpoint the source of the flames.

definitions.definition: Extremely precise or specific, especially regarding location.
definitions.example: The machine assembles parts at high speed and with pinpoint accuracy.

definitions.definition: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
To envelop completely; to surround; to cover; to hide.
definitions.example: to involve in darkness or obscurity

definitions.definition: To complicate or make intricate, as in grammatical structure.
To connect with something as a natural or logical consequence or effect; to include necessarily; to imply.
To take in; to gather in; to mingle confusedly; to blend or merge.
To envelop, enfold, entangle.
definitions.example: He's involved in the crime.

definitions.definition: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.
definitions.example: By getting involved in her local community, Mary met lots of people and also helped make it a nicer place to live.

definitions.definition: To raise to any assigned power; to multiply, as a quantity, into itself a given number of times.
definitions.example: a quantity involved to the third or fourth power

definitions.definition: To call upon (a person, a god) for help, assistance or guidance.
To solicit, petition for, appeal to a favorable attitude.
definitions.example: The envoy invoked the King of Kings's magnanimity to reduce his province's tribute after another drought.

definitions.definition: To call to mind (something) for some purpose.
To appeal for validation to a (notably cited) authority.
definitions.example: In certain Christian circles, invoking the Bible constitutes irrefutable proof.

definitions.definition: To conjure up with incantations.
definitions.example: This satanist ritual invokes Beelzebub.

definitions.definition: To bring about as an inevitable consequence.
definitions.example: Blasphemy is taboo as it may invoke divine wrath.

definitions.definition: To cause (a program or subroutine) to execute.
definitions.example: Interactive programs let the users enter choices and invoke the corresponding routines.

definitions.definition: To overlap mutually, so that each partially covers the other.
The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest discrete, meaningful unit of language. (contrast morpheme.)
Something like such a unit of language:
The fact or act of speaking, as opposed to taking action. .
Something that someone said; a comment, utterance; speech.
A watchword or rallying cry, a verbal signal (even when consisting of multiple words).
definitions.example: mum's the word

definitions.definition: A proverb or motto.
News; tidings (used without an article).
definitions.example: Have you had any word from John yet?

definitions.definition: An order; a request or instruction; an expression of will.
definitions.example: Don't fire till I give the word

definitions.definition: A promise; an oath or guarantee.
definitions.example: I give you my word that I will be there on time.

definitions.definition: A brief discussion or conversation.
definitions.example: Can I have a word with you?

definitions.definition: (in the plural) See words.
definitions.example: There had been words between him and the secretary about the outcome of the meeting.

definitions.definition: (sometimes Word) Communication from God; the message of the Christian gospel; the Bible, Scripture.
definitions.example: Her parents had lived in Botswana, spreading the word among the tribespeople.

definitions.definition: (sometimes Word) Logos, Christ.
To say or write (something) using particular words; to phrase (something).
definitions.example: I’m not sure how to word this letter to the council.

definitions.definition: To flatter with words, to cajole.
To ply or overpower with words.
To conjure with a word.
To speak, to use words; to converse, to discourse.
Angry debate or conversation; argument.
definitions.example: After she found out the truth, she had words with him, to tell him how she felt.

definitions.definition: Lines in a script for a performance.
definitions.example: You better get your words memorised before rehearsal next Saturday.

definitions.definition: Lyrics.
definitions.example: The composer set the words to music.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of a woman; feminine; effeminate.
Carried out by or pertaining to a woman.
An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.
Something that concentrates in itself the qualities of a larger item, or multiple items.
An abstraction; an abstract term; that which is abstract.
The theoretical way of looking at things; something that exists only in idealized form.
An abstract work of art.
A summary title of the key points detailing a tract of land, for ownership; abstract of title.
To separate; to disengage.
To remove; to take away; withdraw.
To steal; to take away; to remove without permission.
To summarize; to abridge; to epitomize.

To conceptualize an ideal subgroup by means of the generalization of an attribute, as follows: by apprehending an attribute inherent to one individual, then separating that attribute and contemplating it by itself, then conceiving of that attribute as a general quality, then despecifying that conceived quality with respect to several or many individuals, and by then ideating a group composed of those individuals perceived to possess said quality.
To extract by means of distillation.
To consider abstractly; to contemplate separately or by itself; to consider theoretically; to look at as a general quality.
To withdraw oneself; to retire.
To draw off (interest or attention).
definitions.example: He was wholly abstracted by other objects.

definitions.definition: To perform the process of abstraction.
To create abstractions.
To produce an abstraction, usually by refactoring existing code. Generally used with "out".
definitions.example: He abstracted out the square root function.

definitions.definition: Derived; extracted.
Drawn away; removed from; apart from; separate.
Not concrete: conceptual, ideal.
Difficult to understand; abstruse; hard to conceptualize.
Separately expressing a property or attribute of an object that is considered to be inherent to that object: attributive, ascriptive.

Pertaining comprehensively to, or representing, a class or group of objects, as opposed to any specific object; considered apart from any application to a particular object: general, generic, nonspecific; representational.
Absent-minded.
Pertaining to the formal aspect of art, such as the lines, colors, shapes, and the relationships among them.
Insufficiently factual.
Apart from practice or reality; vague; theoretical; impersonal; not applied.
(grammar) As a noun, denoting an intangible as opposed to an object, place, or person.
Of a class in object-oriented programming, being a partial basis for subclasses rather than a complete template for objects.
To expel (one or more people) from their property; to force (one or more people) to move out.
One who gardens; one who grows plants or cultivates a garden.
definitions.example: "Ponder the fact that God has made you a gardener, to root out vice and to plant virtue." — St. Catherine of Siena

definitions.definition: (of a person) The quality of speaking or stating the truth; truthfulness.
Something that is true; a truthful statement; a truth.
Agreement with the facts; accordance with the truth; accuracy or precision.
Act of being exact and accurate.
Correctness and carefulness in one's plan of action.
A device in a television camera that forms an image composed of varying charges on a photoconductive surface
A submicroscopic, non-cellular structure consisting of a core of DNA or RNA surrounded by a protein coat, that requires a living host cell to replicate, and often causes disease in the host organism.
A disease caused by these organisms.
definitions.example: He caught a virus and had to stay home from school.

definitions.definition: Venom, as produced by a poisonous animal etc.

A type of malware which can covertly transmit itself between computers via networks (especially the Internet) or removable storage such as disks, often causing damage to systems and data; also computer virus.
Any type of malware.
One who, or that which, lights.
definitions.example: a lighter of lamps

definitions.definition: A small, reusable handheld device for creating fire, especially for lighting cigarettes.
definitions.example: Cigarette in mouth, he clutched his pockets in search of a lighter.

definitions.definition: A flat-bottomed boat for carrying heavy loads across short distances (especially for canals or for loading or unloading larger boats).
To tell in a descriptive way.
definitions.example: Please relate the circumstances of your journey here today.

definitions.definition: To bring into a relation, association, or connection (between one thing and another).
To have a connection.
definitions.example: The patterns on the screen relate to the pitch and volume of the music being played.

definitions.definition: To interact.
To respond through reaction.
(with to) To identify with; to understand.
definitions.example: I find it difficult to relate to others because I'm extremely introverted.

definitions.definition: To bring back; to restore.
Standing in relation or connection.
definitions.example: Electric and magnetic forces are closely related.

definitions.definition: Being a relative of.
definitions.example: Everyone is related to their parents.

definitions.definition: Narrated; told.
Fulfilling a relation.
(in combination) Having a relationship with the thing named
definitions.example: Gun-related crime.

definitions.definition: Connected to or depending on something else; comparative.
(of a URL, URI, path, or similar) Expressed in relation to another item, rather than in complete form.
definitions.example: The relative URL /images/pic.jpg, when evaluated in the context of http://example.com/docs/pic.html, corresponds to the absolute URL http://example.com/images/pic.jpg.

definitions.definition: (grammar) That relates to an antecedent.
Having the same key but differing in being major or minor.
Relevant; pertinent; related.
definitions.example: relative to your earlier point about taxes, ...

definitions.definition: Capable to be changed by other beings or circumstance; conditional.
To renovate, revise, improve or renew.
definitions.example: They plan to revamp the historical theater in the old downtown.

definitions.definition: All the electromagnetic radiation given off by the Sun, especially that in the visible spectrum that bathes the Earth.
definitions.example: Sunlight on the skin gives you vitamin D.

definitions.definition: Brightness, hope; a positive outlook.
To work on the side (at a secondary job) during the daytime.
The time of day when the sun appears above the eastern horizon.
definitions.example: I'll meet you at the docks at sunrise.

definitions.definition: The change in color of the sky at dawn.
definitions.example: Did you see the beautiful sunrise this morning?

definitions.definition: Any great awakening.
definitions.example: It was the sunrise of her spirit.

definitions.definition: To cause (something to have) the streaked or swirled appearance of certain types of marble, for example by mixing viscous ingredients incompletely, or by applying paint or other colorants unevenly.
To get or have the streaked or swirled appearance of certain types of marble, for example due to the incomplete mixing of viscous ingredients, or the uneven application of paint or other colorants.
To cause meat, usually beef, pork, or lamb, to be interlaced with fat so that its appearance resembles that of marble.
(of meat, especially beef) To become interlaced with fat; (of fat) to interlace through meat.
(by extension) To lace or be laced throughout.
Composed of marble; having a marble exterior.
Having marbling.
Resembling marble.
(meat) Interlaced with fat.
To surpass or be better than something or someone else
The state of having complete legal control of something; possession; proprietorship.
Responsibility for something.
definitions.example: The successful candidate will take ownership of all internal design projects.

definitions.definition: The act of kowtowing.
Having a kind personality; kind, warmhearted, sympathetic.
definitions.example: A kindly old man sits on the park bench every afternoon feeding pigeons.

definitions.definition: Favourable, gentle, pleasant, tidy, auspicious, beneficent.
Lawful.
Natural; inherent to the kind or race.
An ice yacht.
An icebreaker; a ship that breaks through ice.
Shy; not liking to be noticed; socially timid.
Indicating bashfulness.
definitions.example: a bashful look

definitions.definition: One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
The part of the face below the mouth.
definitions.example: His jaw dropped in amazement.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling the jaw of an animal in form or action; especially plural, the mouth or way of entrance.
definitions.example: the jaws of a pass; the jaws of darkness; the jaws of death.

definitions.definition: A notch or opening.
A notched or forked part, adapted for holding an object in place.
definitions.example: the jaw of a railway-car pedestal.

definitions.definition: One of a pair of opposing parts which are movable towards or from each other, for grasping or crushing anything between them.
definitions.example: the jaws of a vise; the jaws of a stone-crushing machine.

definitions.definition: The inner end of a boom or gaff, hollowed in a half circle so as to move freely on a mast.
Impudent or abusive talk.
Axle guard.
The curved part of the cushion marking the entry to the pocket.
To assail or abuse by scolding.
To scold; to clamor.
To talk; to converse.
(of a ball) To stick in the jaws of a pocket.
The mouth
The borders of anything which has a mouthlike aspect.
definitions.example: Open the jaws of the wrench and wide as possible.

definitions.definition: (eastern Ohio) a contrarian.

A mostly herbivorous insect of the order Orthoptera, noted for its ability to jump long distances and for the habit of some species communicating by stridulation; they are related to but distinct from crickets.
A cocktail made with crème de menthe and optionally with crème de cacao.
A young student in initial stages of training who has been chosen on account of their obvious talent.
In ordinary square or upright pianos of London make, the escapement lever or jack, so made that it can be taken out and replaced with the key.
A drawing or picture.
(mostly in plural) A computer-generated image as viewed on a screen forming part of a game or a film etc.
definitions.example: I've just played this new computer game: the graphics are amazing.

definitions.definition: Drawn, pictorial.
Vivid, descriptive, often in relation to depictions of sex or violence.
Having a texture that resembles writing, commonly created by exsolution, devitrification and immiscibility processes in igneous rocks.
definitions.example: graphic granite

definitions.definition: A personal digital assistant or video game console that is small enough to be held in the hands.
Held in one or both hands.
Small and light enough to be operated while held in one or both hands.
(grammar) A verb form which does not function as a predicate, or a word derived from a verb. In English, infinitives, participles and gerunds are verbals.
A confession given to police.
To induce into fabricating a confession.
Of or relating to words.
Concerned with the words, rather than the substance of a text.
Consisting of words only.
Expressly spoken rather than written; oral.
definitions.example: a verbal contract

definitions.definition: (grammar) Derived from, or having the nature of a verb.
(grammar) Used to form a verb.
Capable of speech.
Word for word.
definitions.example: a verbal translation

definitions.definition: Abounding with words; verbose.
The night office of the Christian liturgy of the Hours, such as is performed in monasteries.
A portion of the psalter used during nocturns.
Momentous, significant, setting or sealing one’s fate.
definitions.example: It started with that fateful trip, history was never the same afterwards.

definitions.definition: Determined in advance by fate, fated.
One who finds or discovers something.
An optical device, such as a viewfinder, used to locate a target or other object of interest
A mountain lion; Puma concolor.
An older woman who actively seeks the casual, often sexual, companionship of younger men, by implication a female “sexual predator”.
definitions.example: A cougar approached Warren at the Palomino Club and asked for a dance.

definitions.definition: An obstacle, delay, disadvantage, blow (an adverse event which retards or prevents progress towards a desired outcome)
definitions.example: After some initial setbacks, the expedition went safely on its way.

definitions.definition: The required distance between a structure and a road.
A step-like recession in a wall.
definitions.example: Setbacks were initially used for structural reasons, but now are often mandated by land use codes.

definitions.definition: An offset to the temperature setting of a thermostat to cover a period when more or less heating is required than usual.
(possibly obsolete) A backset; a countercurrent; an eddy.
A backset; a check; a repulse; a relapse.
A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or concealment.
definitions.example: Daniel was put into the lions’ den.

definitions.definition: A squalid or wretched place; a haunt.
definitions.example: a den of vice

definitions.definition: A comfortable room not used for formal entertaining.
A narrow glen; a ravine; a dell.
To ensconce or hide oneself in (or as in) a den.
The hymn portions of the Vedas; any passage of these used as a prayer.
(originally Hinduism) A phrase repeated to assist concentration during meditation.
(by extension) A slogan or phrase often repeated.
A type of confectionery made by boiling sugar (or treacle, etc) with butter or milk, then cooling the mixture so that it becomes hard
A small, individual piece of toffee
definitions.example: a box of toffees

definitions.definition: Any kind of sweets; candy
To coat in toffee.
The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
Something that is useful.
The ability of a commodity to satisfy needs or wants; the satisfaction experienced by the consumer of that commodity.
Well-being, satisfaction, pleasure, or happiness.
A service provider, such as an electric company or water company; or, the securities of such a provider.
A software program designed to perform a single task or a small range of tasks, often to help manage and tune computer hardware, an operating system or application software.
definitions.example: I've bought a new disk utility that can recover deleted files.

definitions.definition: The ability to play multiple positions.
To undo or open a lock or something locked by, for example, turning a key, or selecting a combination.
definitions.example: I unlocked the door and walked in.

definitions.definition: To obtain access to something.
definitions.example: I unlocked the dictionary article so I could edit it.

definitions.definition: To disclose or reveal previously unknown knowledge.
definitions.example: The discovery of a clue unlocked the mystery.

definitions.definition: To be or become unfastened or unrestrained.
Not secured or protected by a lock.
definitions.example: an unlocked computer file

definitions.definition: (of a phone) Not bound to any particular carrier.
definitions.example: an unlocked iPhone

definitions.definition: A bird in the genus Cephalopterus, found in rainforests of Central and South America, with a conspicuous crest on the top of their head, vaguely resembling an umbrella.
The flat, roundish bone in the knee.
(roofing) A metal cover trim that fits over a panel rib after it has been cut and bent.
A cap or strong covering for the knees, used chiefly for horses, to protect their knees in case of a fall.
To destroy the knees of (a person), usually by shooting at the knees, as a punishment carried out by criminals or terrorists.
To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).
definitions.example: Buying more servers would improve performance.

definitions.definition: To become better.
definitions.example: I have improved since taking the tablets.

definitions.definition: To disprove or make void; to refute.
To disapprove of; to find fault with; to reprove; to censure.
definitions.example: to improve negligence

definitions.definition: To use or employ to good purpose; to turn to profitable account.
definitions.example: to improve one's time;  to improve his means

definitions.definition: That has been made better; enhanced.
Sweet potato (the plant).
To draw blood from one's opponent in a fight.
To demonstrably harm the cause of an opponent.
Covered in blood.
definitions.example: All that remained of his right hand after the accident was a bloody stump.

definitions.definition: Characterised by bloodshed.
definitions.example: There have been bloody battles between the two tribes.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier.
Badly behaved; unpleasant; beastly.
(intensifier) Used to express anger, annoyance, shock, or for emphasis.
With zealous fervor; excited, motivated.
definitions.example: an enthusiastic lover of art

definitions.definition: To come into view.
To come out of a situation, object or a liquid.
definitions.example: He emerged unscathed from the accident.

definitions.definition: To become known.
definitions.example: Gradually the truth emerged.

definitions.definition: To pull with a jerk.
definitions.example: She hitched her jeans up and then tightened her belt.

definitions.definition: To attach, tie or fasten.
definitions.example: He hitched the bedroll to his backpack and went camping.

definitions.definition: To marry oneself to; especially to get hitched.
Contraction of hitchhike, to thumb a ride.
definitions.example: to hitch a ride

definitions.definition: To become entangled or caught; to be linked or yoked; to unite; to cling.
To move interruptedly or with halts, jerks, or steps; said of something obstructed or impeded.
To strike the legs together in going, as horses; to interfere.
A person who wanders from place to place; a nomad, a wanderer.
(specifically) A person without settled employment or habitation who supports himself or herself by begging or some dishonest means; a tramp, a vagabond.
definitions.example: Every morning before work, I see that poor vagrant around the neighbourhood begging for food.

definitions.definition: Vagrans egista, a widely distributed Asian butterfly of the family Nymphalidae.
An animal, typically a bird, found outside its species' usual range.
To hunt fish or other aquatic animals.
definitions.example: She went to the river to fish for trout.

definitions.definition: To search (a body of water) for something other than fish.
definitions.example: They fished the surrounding lakes for the dead body.

definitions.definition: To use as bait when fishing.
To (attempt to) find or get hold of an object by searching among other objects.
definitions.example: He was fishing for the keys in his pocket.

definitions.definition: (followed by "for" or "around for") To talk to people in an attempt to get them to say something, or seek to obtain something by artifice.
definitions.example: The actors loitered at the door, fishing for compliments.

definitions.definition: Of a batsman, to attempt to hit a ball outside off stump and miss it.
To repair (a spar or mast) by fastening a beam or other long object (often called a fish) over the damaged part (see Noun above).
To hoist the flukes of.
A migratory route used by birds between breeding areas.
The branch of natural science that deals with the composition and constitution of substances and the changes that they undergo as a consequence of alterations in the constitution of their molecules.
An application of chemical theory and method to a particular substance.
The mutual attraction between two people; rapport.
Canis latrans, a species of canine native to North America.
A smuggler of undocumented immigrants across the land border from Latin America into the United States of America.
To prospect for gold by manually digging holes into overlying earth, as into a hillside.
A sudden resistance or refusal.
definitions.example: He was surprised by her quick rebuff to his proposal.

definitions.definition: Repercussion, or beating back.
To refuse; to offer sudden or harsh resistance; to turn down or shut out.
To buff again.
